project.name: consulting-revenue-platform-api
project.description: V2 backlog focused on architecture hardening, transactional integrity, and showcase-ready productization.

owner_profile_context.target_outcome: Portfolio-quality backend project that demonstrates direct business impact and production readiness.

execution.recommended_method: Implement one epic at a time in dependency order, preserving backwards-compatible API behavior while extracting clearer domain boundaries.
definition_of_done: All V2 stories for the active epic are marked done with acceptance criteria satisfied.
CI checks pass: fmt, unit tests, race tests, lint, migration checks, and build.
OpenAPI and runbook remain aligned with runtime behavior.
Critical money and workflow paths have explicit transaction-boundary tests.
global_constraints: All tenant-scoped business tables include organization_id.
All tenant-scoped queries enforce organization_id filtering.
Mutating endpoints require authentication and authorization.
Money values are stored as integer minor units; no floating-point arithmetic for currency.
All logs must avoid secrets and unnecessary PII.
Standard API error envelope is used everywhere.
Refactors must preserve existing route paths and auth expectations unless story scope explicitly states otherwise.

agent_rules: Do not proceed on a story until dependencies are complete.
Keep pull requests scoped to one story whenever practical.
For refactors, preserve behavior with targeted regression tests.
When uncertain, favor secure defaults and explicit validation.

categories.name: Domain and Use-Case Architecture
categories.description: Refactor critical business workflows into explicit domain/use-case layers while keeping API contracts stable.

categories.name: Transactional Integrity and Operability
categories.description: Explicit transaction boundaries for money-critical workflows, rollback proofs, RED metrics, structured logging, and operator runbooks.

categories.name: Stripe Integration and Payment Reliability
categories.description: Harden Stripe webhooks, payment links, and reconciliation with tests and documentation aligned with observability from Epic K.

categories.name: API Contract Governance
categories.description: Prevent OpenAPI/runtime drift with contract-focused tests, release checks, and compatibility policies.

categories.name: Demo Experience and Showcase UX
categories.description: Deliver a polished, reproducible demo flow with optional lightweight frontend and scripted evidence for portfolio presentation.

stories.title: Define domain boundaries and interfaces for time-entry workflow
stories.description: Introduce explicit domain model and repository interfaces for time-entry lifecycle operations without changing external API contracts.
scope: Create domain package types for time-entry status and transition invariants.
Define repository interfaces required by time-entry submit, approve, and reject use cases.
Add architecture notes describing new package boundaries and dependency direction.
out_of_scope: Migrating invoicing logic in this story.
implementation_notes: Keep domain package free of database and HTTP imports.
Model transition guards as typed errors instead of string matching.
security_privacy: Role and tenant checks remain enforced before use-case execution.
edge_cases: Reject requires reason; preserve existing validation behavior.
Submitted/approved state conflicts remain deterministic.
acceptance_criteria: New domain and interface packages compile and are used by time-entry workflow path.
Behavior for submit/approve/reject endpoints is unchanged for success and error cases.
Tests verify domain transition matrix independent of HTTP transport.
test_plan: Add unit tests for domain transition rules.
Run existing HTTP authz and workflow tests to confirm no regression.
deliverables: Domain types and repository interfaces for time-entry lifecycle.
Short architecture doc update for dependency boundaries.

stories.title: Extract time-entry workflow into explicit application use-case layer
stories.description: Move time-entry submit, approve, and reject orchestration out of handlers into a dedicated use-case service.
scope: Implement application service for submit, approve, and reject flows.
Refactor handlers to parse/authorize and delegate to use-case layer.
Preserve audit event semantics for transition actions.
out_of_scope: Changing endpoint paths, auth headers, or status codes.
implementation_notes: Use constructor-based dependency injection for use-case service.
Map typed domain errors to existing HTTP error envelope in one adapter layer.
security_privacy: Authorization decisions stay in edge layer before use-case invocation.
edge_cases: Idempotent conflict responses remain consistent for already transitioned entries.
Contractor self-ownership restriction remains enforced.
acceptance_criteria: Handlers for submit/approve/reject contain no direct repository transition calls.
All existing workflow endpoint tests pass unchanged or with only adapter-level updates.
Audit log actions remain emitted for successful transitions.
test_plan: Add use-case unit tests with in-memory or mock repositories.
Run internal/httpapi workflow and authz regression tests.
deliverables: Application use-case package for time-entry transitions.
Refactored handlers delegating to use-case service.

stories.title: Refactor invoice generation and send into use-case services with explicit transaction boundaries
stories.description: Consolidate invoice generation and send orchestration into use-case services and formalize transaction boundaries for multi-step writes.
scope: Introduce use-case services for invoice generate and send operations.
Use explicit transaction wrapper for multi-table invoice workflows.
Keep current response schema and route contract intact.
out_of_scope: Stripe payment-link and webhook reconciliation refactor.
implementation_notes: Make transaction ownership explicit in use-case layer, not handlers.
Preserve deterministic invoice totals and status transition semantics.
security_privacy: Tenant scoping and invoice write-role checks must remain enforced.
edge_cases: No eligible approved entries returns existing conflict behavior.
Send on non-draft invoice returns conflict without side effects.
acceptance_criteria: Invoice generate and send handlers delegate orchestration to use-case layer.
Transaction tests prove partial failure does not persist inconsistent invoice/time-entry state.
Existing endpoint response payload fields remain unchanged.
test_plan: Add integration tests for transaction rollback on injected failures.
Run current invoice API/authz tests and repo tests.
deliverables: Use-case service for invoice generation and send.

stories.title: Standardize typed domain errors and transport mapping across refactored flows
stories.description: Replace fragile string-based branching with typed errors and a centralized transport mapping layer for time-entry and invoice workflows.
scope: Define typed domain/application errors for conflict, validation, and not-found cases.
Add shared mapping helper from domain errors to API error envelope.
Apply mapping in refactored time-entry and invoice handlers.
out_of_scope: Global rewrite of every handler in the codebase.
implementation_notes: Use errors.Is-compatible sentinel/typed errors to avoid brittle substring checks.
Keep error code/message outputs stable for existing API consumers.
security_privacy: Do not expose internal stack or SQL details in client-facing errors.
edge_cases: Unknown errors still map to internal_error with request_id context.
Validation conflicts preserve expected HTTP status classes.
acceptance_criteria: Refactored handlers no longer branch on string-contains for expected business errors.
Error mapping tests cover known domain errors to HTTP code/code/message combinations.
External API error envelope format remains unchanged.
test_plan: Add table-driven tests for domain-to-transport error mapping.
Run existing endpoint regression tests for affected routes.
deliverables: Typed error definitions for refactored flows.
Shared transport error mapper used by handlers.

stories.title: Document architecture boundaries and contribution rules for domain/use-case/repo layers
stories.description: Add concise contributor guidance so future stories follow the new dependency direction and avoid relapsing into mixed-layer logic.
scope: Document layer responsibilities: transport, use-case, domain, repository.
Add concrete examples of where validation, authz, and transactions should live.
Reference these rules in README and runbook contribution sections.
out_of_scope: Organization-wide process docs outside this repository.
implementation_notes: Keep documentation actionable and under one page where possible.
Include migration guidance for remaining unrefactored handlers.
security_privacy: Document secure defaults for boundary placement (authz at transport edge, secrets never logged).
edge_cases: Contributors touching legacy paths need guidance to avoid half-refactors.
acceptance_criteria: Repository docs clearly define dependency direction and examples.
README includes a quick-start note for adding new use-case services.
Story J implementation areas are explicitly mapped to new boundaries.
test_plan: Manual documentation walkthrough validating that a new contributor can place code correctly.
Link check for any added internal docs references.
deliverables: Architecture guidance document for V2 layering model.
README/runbook updates referencing the new boundaries.

stories.title: Define transaction-boundary matrix and shared RunInTx helper for critical workflows
stories.description: Document where money-critical transactions begin and end; centralize commit/rollback in internal/db.RunInTx and apply across repository boundaries.
scope: Add docs/transaction-matrix.md mapping routes to repository transaction owners.
Implement db.RunInTx and refactor critical repo paths (time entry workflow, invoice generation/send, registration).
Link matrix from architecture-boundaries.md.
out_of_scope: Changing public API paths or payloads.
implementation_notes: Use-case layer calls single repo operations that wrap one transaction each.
security_privacy: Rollback paths must not log sensitive payloads.
edge_cases: Nested transaction misuse should fail or no-op deterministically.
acceptance_criteria: Transaction matrix exists and references RunInTx usage.
Critical workflows use shared transaction helper at repo layer.
test_plan: Unit tests for RunInTx success, rollback, panic recovery.

stories.title: Rollback-focused integration tests for invoice and time-entry flows
stories.description: Postgres integration tests inject failures mid-transaction and assert no partial writes remain.
scope: Tests for time entry submit, invoice generation, and invoice send failure paths.
CI provides DATABASE_URL and migrations for integrationtest package.
implementation_notes: Prefer triggers or controlled injection in Postgres over sqlmock for rollback guarantees.
security_privacy: Tests use synthetic tenants and emails only.
edge_cases: Sequence allocation rollback behavior documented if applicable.
acceptance_criteria: Integration tests fail if partial rows persist after injected errors.
go test ./... runs them in CI with Postgres service.
test_plan: go test ./internal/integrationtest/... with migrated database.

stories.title: Route-level RED metrics and business workflow conflict counters
stories.description: Expose stable http_requests_total with status_class labels and business_workflow_conflict_total for invoice and time-entry conflicts.
scope: Refine HTTP RED metrics in observability middleware.
Increment conflict counters on 409 workflow outcomes.
Document PromQL examples in runbook.
implementation_notes: Keep label cardinality low; never label by tenant or user id.
security_privacy: Metrics must not include secrets or PII.
edge_cases: Unmatched routes still get stable route label.
acceptance_criteria: RED-style labels documented; conflict counters exported for critical workflows.
test_plan: Unit tests for status_class; integration scrape of /metrics.

stories.title: Structured logging field contract and worker correlation IDs
stories.description: Document logging fields; add correlation_id to webhook and retention workers; enrich panic logs with route.
scope: docs/logging.md field contract.
slog JSON for workers with component and correlation_id.
implementation_notes: Reuse request_id from middleware for HTTP; generate per-poll correlation for workers.
security_privacy: Never log tokens or Authorization headers.
edge_cases: Background work without HTTP request still has a correlation key.
acceptance_criteria: Critical paths documented; workers emit structured JSON with correlation_id.
test_plan: Regression on log redaction tests if present.

stories.title: Operability runbook updates for transactions and incidents
stories.description: Expand runbook with transaction triage, metrics to check, and PR verification checklist for operability changes.
scope: Incident steps for rollback anomalies and stuck workflows.
Link transaction matrix to tests; local PR checklist.
implementation_notes: Reference make targets and migrate commands.
security_privacy: Examples use synthetic data only.
edge_cases: Runbook steps should include an operator fallback when integration-test database access is unavailable.
acceptance_criteria: Runbook is concise and command-oriented; links to metrics and matrix.
test_plan: Manual review of runbook accuracy.

stories.title: Stripe webhook processing map and typed failure metrics
stories.description: Document each Stripe event type handled, outcomes, and emit low-cardinality counters for success, retry, and permanent failure paths.
scope: Add docs/stripe-webhook-matrix.md mapping event types to handlers and ledger effects.
Export Prometheus counters for webhook processing outcomes (labels: event_category, outcome).
Wire increments in webhookworker processing path without logging secrets.
out_of_scope: Supporting every Stripe event type in the catalog.
implementation_notes: Align metric names with existing /metrics conventions; keep labels bounded.
security_privacy: Never increment metrics with raw event ids as labels; no card data in logs.
edge_cases: Unknown event types bucket to a single outcome label.
acceptance_criteria: Matrix doc exists and matches code paths.
At least one new outcome counter series is visible on /metrics after tests.
test_plan: Unit tests for metric label bucketing; optional scrape test.
deliverables: Webhook metrics in internal/webhookworker or httpapi

stories.title: Idempotent payment-link and invoice payment mutations
stories.description: Ensure duplicate client requests for payment link creation or payment-state transitions do not double-charge or corrupt state.
scope: Define idempotency strategy (request key header and/or natural keys) for payment-link endpoints.
Repository-level guards for duplicate Stripe payment link creation per invoice when already pending or paid.
Typed conflict responses preserved for API consumers.
implementation_notes: Prefer existing invoice state machine; use use-case layer for orchestration.
security_privacy: Idempotency keys are opaque hashes in logs, not raw client secrets.
edge_cases: Concurrent duplicate POSTs return same outcome or deterministic conflict.
acceptance_criteria: Tests prove duplicate submissions do not create duplicate Stripe resources inappropriately.
OpenAPI documents optional Idempotency-Key where implemented.
test_plan: Concurrency or replay tests at repo/use-case layer.

stories.title: Reconciliation health checks and ledger alignment assertions
stories.description: Add read-only checks that Stripe-reported amounts align with invoice and ledger rows for a tenant scope.
scope: Define reconciliation query or job that flags drift between invoices/payments and ledger_entries for Stripe-backed events.
Expose a protected internal or admin-only summary endpoint or CLI output for operators.
Document interpretation in runbook.
out_of_scope: Automatic money movement or Stripe refunds from this job.
implementation_notes: Scope by organization_id; read-only SQL; suitable for cron.
security_privacy: Admin or owner role only; no raw Stripe objects in responses.
edge_cases: Partial webhook backlog should not false-positive critical alerts without tuning.
acceptance_criteria: Drift detection logic covered by tests with synthetic fixtures.
Runbook explains how to respond to reported drift.
test_plan: Repo or integration tests with seeded rows.

stories.title: Stripe webhook integration test suite with fixture replay
stories.description: Add tests that replay signed or test-mode Stripe event payloads against the webhook handler to prevent regressions.
scope: Golden JSON fixtures for primary event types (payment success, failure, dispute if supported).
Tests exercise signature validation path and persistence to webhook_events plus downstream processing hooks.
CI runs tests without live Stripe network calls.
implementation_notes: Use Stripe test secrets from env in CI only via secrets; default skip without credentials if needed.
security_privacy: Fixtures contain no real customer data.
edge_cases: Clock skew and replay idempotency covered at least once.
acceptance_criteria: At least three event types covered by fixture tests.
go test ./... passes in CI.
test_plan: httptest against webhook handler with synthetic signatures.

stories.title: OpenAPI and showcase documentation for payment surfaces
stories.description: Keep docs/openapi.yaml and README aligned with Stripe webhook, payment link, and reconciliation endpoints added in Epic L.
scope: Update OpenAPI for any new or changed routes in L-02 and L-03.
README quick-start for testing webhooks locally (stripe CLI or documented tunnel).
Cross-link stripe-webhook-matrix and threat model where relevant.
implementation_notes: CI openapi-spec-validator must pass.
security_privacy: Document that webhook secrets stay in environment variables.
edge_cases: README quick-start should include a non-Stripe fallback path when webhook forwarding tools are unavailable.
acceptance_criteria: OpenAPI validates in CI; README steps are copy-pasteable.
test_plan: make openapi-validate or CI schema-validate job.

stories.title: Define API compatibility policy and versioning rules for contract changes
stories.description: Establish explicit compatibility guarantees and change-management rules so API evolution is predictable for consumers.
scope: Create a compatibility policy covering additive vs breaking changes for endpoints, fields, and enums.
Document deprecation strategy, communication expectations, and rollout sequencing.
Link policy in README and runbook where API changes are described.
implementation_notes: Keep policy concise and enforceable in code review.
Align policy language with existing OpenAPI and CI workflows.
security_privacy: Compatibility policy must not encourage exposing sensitive fields for backwards compatibility.
edge_cases: Enum expansion behavior should be documented for clients that assume closed sets.
Error-envelope changes require explicit compatibility handling.
acceptance_criteria: Repository docs include explicit API compatibility and deprecation policy.
Contribution guidance references policy as a required check for API-touching PRs.
Policy includes clear examples of additive, behavior-changing, and breaking updates.
test_plan: Manual policy walkthrough with at least three representative API change scenarios.
Link checks for documentation cross-references.

stories.title: Add OpenAPI route coverage test to detect undocumented runtime endpoints
stories.description: Introduce automated verification that implemented routes are represented in OpenAPI, with explicit allowance list for internal-only endpoints.
scope: Build test tooling that enumerates registered HTTP routes and compares against OpenAPI paths/methods.
Introduce explicit allowlist for intentionally undocumented routes (e.g., internal metrics).
Fail CI when public runtime routes are missing from OpenAPI.
out_of_scope: Runtime auth-behavior verification for each endpoint.
implementation_notes: Normalize path parameter naming differences before comparison.
Keep allowlist small and explicitly justified in code comments/docs.
security_privacy: Undocumented internal routes must be explicitly tagged to avoid accidental public exposure.
edge_cases: Equivalent route patterns with different parameter names should compare as equal.
Method-specific mismatches must fail even if path exists for another method.
acceptance_criteria: CI fails when runtime public route is absent from OpenAPI.
Intentional undocumented routes are tracked in explicit allowlist.
Contributor docs explain how to update OpenAPI and coverage tests together.
test_plan: Unit tests for route normalization and diff logic.
Integration test proving mismatch produces deterministic failure output.
deliverables: OpenAPI route coverage test and allowlist mechanism.

stories.title: Add response-envelope contract tests for critical endpoints
stories.description: Verify that critical endpoint responses conform to OpenAPI schemas for both success and representative error cases.
scope: Select high-impact endpoints across auth, time-entry workflow, invoices, and reports.
Add tests that execute handlers and validate response JSON shape against OpenAPI component/operation schemas.
Cover both success and at least one validation or conflict error per selected endpoint.
out_of_scope: Exhaustive schema validation for every endpoint in one story.
implementation_notes: Prefer deterministic fixture setup to keep contract tests stable.
Use schema references from docs/openapi.yaml as source of truth.
security_privacy: Contract fixtures must use synthetic data only.
Error contract checks must ensure no sensitive internal details leak.
edge_cases: Optional fields omitted in responses should still pass schema validation.
Error responses with details object should validate additionalProperties behavior.
acceptance_criteria: Critical endpoint responses are validated against OpenAPI in automated tests.
At least one success and one error response are checked per selected route.
Contract test failures provide actionable mismatch output.
test_plan: Run contract tests in CI with deterministic fixtures.
Negative test ensures intentionally malformed response payload fails schema validation.
deliverables: OpenAPI-backed response contract test suite for critical endpoints.
Test utility helpers for schema lookup and validation.

stories.title: Enforce OpenAPI change gate in CI with compatibility classification
stories.description: Add CI logic that requires explicit compatibility classification when OpenAPI changes are introduced.
scope: Detect OpenAPI diff in pull requests and classify as additive, behavior-changing, or breaking.
Require PR annotation/check input declaring compatibility class for OpenAPI-touching changes.
Fail CI for unclassified or policy-violating breaking changes.
implementation_notes: Keep classification mechanism lightweight (labels, PR template field, or checked metadata file).
Provide clear CI error messages with remediation steps.
security_privacy: CI metadata should not include secrets or environment-specific URLs.
edge_cases: Non-functional doc edits in OpenAPI should be classified as non-breaking automatically when possible.
Simultaneous endpoint add/remove should require explicit breaking-change acknowledgement.
acceptance_criteria: CI enforces compatibility classification when OpenAPI file changes.
Breaking changes are blocked unless explicitly permitted by policy.
PR template or contribution docs include classification instructions.
test_plan: Workflow test with sample PR metadata simulating additive and breaking diffs.
Manual dry-run of classification script against representative OpenAPI diffs.
deliverables: CI workflow step for OpenAPI compatibility gate.

stories.title: Publish API governance runbook for contract changes and release readiness
stories.description: Document repeatable release-readiness checks and governance workflow for API contract changes.
scope: Add runbook section for contract-change checklist, including OpenAPI update, parity test, compatibility classification, and communication notes.
Define release-readiness command sequence for local verification.
Add examples of acceptable additive changes and forbidden unannounced breaking changes.
implementation_notes: Keep checklist concise and copy/paste friendly for pull request authors.
Reference existing CI gates rather than duplicating policy text.
security_privacy: Governance docs should avoid leaking internal-only endpoint details outside intended docs.
edge_cases: Runbook should include fallback steps if contract tests are flaky or unavailable.
Document handling for emergency fixes requiring temporary policy exceptions.
acceptance_criteria: Runbook includes end-to-end governance checklist for API contract changes.
README links to governance workflow for contributors.
Checklist aligns with CI gates introduced in Epic M.
test_plan: Manual runbook drill completing checklist for a sample OpenAPI change.
Link and command sanity validation for documented release-readiness steps.
deliverables: Contributor-facing release-readiness checklist for contract updates.

stories.title: Define showcase demo narrative and success criteria
stories.description: Create a structured demo script that maps business value to concrete API flows and measurable outcomes.
scope: Write a concise narrative for portfolio/demo use covering actor personas, key steps, and expected outputs.
Define a canonical happy path (contractor submits time, owner approves, invoice generated/sent, reporting output).
Document objective success criteria for each stage of the demo.
implementation_notes: Keep narrative customer-facing but traceable to actual route behavior and outputs.
Use deterministic data points so demo expectations are testable.
security_privacy: Demo artifacts must use synthetic identities and data only.
edge_cases: Demo should include fallback note if Stripe-dependent steps are not configured.
Narrative should call out expected 4xx failures for role-mismatch scenarios.
acceptance_criteria: A documented showcase flow exists with step-by-step outcomes.
Each demo step maps to concrete API endpoints and expected responses.
Success criteria are objective and verifiable by script output.
test_plan: Manual walkthrough of narrative against local environment.
Peer review of narrative clarity and endpoint mapping accuracy.
deliverables: Demo narrative document with actor-based flow.
Checklist of expected outputs per demo step.

stories.title: Enhance automated demo script with assertions and cleanup mode
stories.description: Upgrade demo automation to provide deterministic pass/fail assertions and optional cleanup for repeatable portfolio runs.
scope: Extend demo script to assert critical response fields and status transitions end-to-end.
Add optional cleanup/reset mode to remove or isolate prior demo artifacts safely.
Emit human-readable summary and machine-readable result artifact (JSON or text report).
implementation_notes: Support idempotent reruns via unique test identifiers and optional reset flags.
Keep script dependency footprint minimal (shell + curl + jq).
security_privacy: Script output must avoid exposing tokens or secret values.
Cleanup mode must constrain deletions to synthetic demo scope.
edge_cases: Partial run failures should return non-zero exit and actionable diagnostics.
Environment without jq should produce clear dependency error.
acceptance_criteria: Demo script exits non-zero on assertion failure and zero on full success.
Optional cleanup mode allows repeatable local demo runs without manual DB surgery.
Script output includes compact success summary suitable for portfolio evidence.
test_plan: Run script in clean DB and rerun with cleanup/reset mode.
Inject intentional mismatch to verify assertion failure behavior.
deliverables: Enhanced demo script with assertions and optional cleanup.
Demo execution report artifact format and sample output.

stories.title: Create a lightweight frontend demo shell for key API workflows
stories.description: Add a minimal frontend that demonstrates login, org context, and critical workflow visibility without full product scope expansion.
scope: Bootstrap a lightweight frontend shell with environment-configured API base URL.
Implement basic screens for auth login, me/context, clients/projects list, and key workflow actions.
Display structured API responses and surfaced errors for demo transparency.
out_of_scope: Production-grade design system and comprehensive UX polish.
Full coverage of every API endpoint.
implementation_notes: Keep architecture intentionally thin: transport DTOs and API client wrappers only.
Use explicit token/org context wiring with clear local-dev instructions.
security_privacy: Document token storage approach and associated security trade-offs.
Restrict CORS configuration to intended local frontend origin(s).
edge_cases: Expired tokens should redirect to login with clear message.
Missing org header context should produce actionable UI error.
acceptance_criteria: Frontend can execute core demo flow against local API with minimal setup.
Users can authenticate and view core tenant-scoped resources.
UI clearly surfaces API errors without exposing sensitive internals.
test_plan: Manual browser test for login and core list/view actions.
Basic frontend build/test command run in CI or documented local checks.
deliverables: Lightweight frontend demo shell with setup instructions.
Core workflow screens and API integration layer.

stories.title: Add demo-quality data bootstrap presets and scenario toggles
stories.description: Provide deterministic seed presets that support multiple showcase scenarios with explicit role and state setup.
scope: Extend seed tooling with named presets (minimal, happy-path, conflict-path).
Add scenario toggles for role distribution and pre-staged workflow states.
Document seed preset usage in README/runbook and demo docs.
implementation_notes: Keep default preset backward-compatible with existing demo behavior.
Ensure preset IDs/emails are deterministic and clearly namespaced.
security_privacy: Presets must contain synthetic placeholder PII only.
Seed logs must avoid printing sensitive tokens or secrets.
edge_cases: Reset mode should fail safely when immutable ledger constraints block cleanup.
Scenario toggles should validate incompatible combinations with clear errors.
acceptance_criteria: Seed command supports at least three named showcase presets.
Each preset produces deterministic expected entities and states.
Docs explain when to use each preset and expected outcomes.
test_plan: Integration tests for each preset on clean DB.
Manual demo verification across happy-path and conflict-path presets.
deliverables: Seed preset support with documented scenario toggles.
Preset validation tests and usage documentation.

stories.title: Publish end-to-end showcase guide with recording checklist
stories.description: Create a polished showcase guide that combines script, frontend, and operational evidence into a repeatable presentation workflow.
scope: Write a concise showcase guide for live demo and recorded walkthrough modes.
Include checklist for pre-demo environment checks, command sequence, and fallback paths.
Add evidence capture checklist (logs, metrics, script output, screenshots) for portfolio artifacts.
out_of_scope: Public website hosting of demo assets.
implementation_notes: Keep guide short enough for rehearsal but complete enough for first-time execution.
Include branch/version pinning guidance to avoid drift during recording.
security_privacy: Checklist must explicitly mask secrets and tokens during recording.
Portfolio artifacts should avoid real identifiers and credentials.
edge_cases: Guide should include fallback plan if Stripe-dependent steps are unavailable.
Local machine resource constraints should have mitigation notes.
acceptance_criteria: Showcase guide supports a complete end-to-end run by a new reviewer.
Recording checklist includes security hygiene and artifact capture requirements.
README links to showcase guide as primary demo entrypoint.
test_plan: Dry-run walkthrough by someone not authoring the guide.
Checklist validation ensuring each step maps to existing script/frontend capability.
deliverables: End-to-end showcase guide for live and recorded demos.
